$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

get.description: Returns the health status of the application
Returns whether the application is ready to accept requests
Platform admin read over the compliance audit trail. Supports filtering by tenant, actor, action, resource, outcome, and time range.

parameters.description: Action family prefix (e.g. auth. to match all auth.* events)
parameters.name: actionPrefix

schema.description: Action family prefix (e.g. auth. to match all auth.* events)

get.description: Returns every client_users row joined with tenant memberships. Self-registered users with no memberships are included with an empty memberships array.
get.summary: List Tier-2 client users with their tenant memberships

post.description: Inserts a client_users row with the supplied password (validated against the live policy) and EmailVerified=true so the user can log in immediately. For an alternate flow that emails a token instead of needing a password, use POST /v1/admin/client-users/invite.
post.summary: Admin-direct create of a Tier-2 client user

post.description: Creates the client_users row with no password, emails an admin_invite token. The recipient redeems it via /v1/auth/client/accept-invite to set their password and verify the email in one step.
post.summary: Invite a new Tier-2 client user via email

delete.description: Calls SoftDeleteAndAliasEmail so the original address can be reused for a fresh signup. The original email is preserved on the user document for audit. Requires a fresh MFA proof (RequireStepUp).
delete.summary: Soft-delete a Tier-2 client user and free its email

get.summary: Get a single Tier-2 client user with tenant memberships

patch.description: Patches name/username/email/phone/role/isActive. Requires a fresh MFA proof (RequireStepUp).
patch.summary: Update profile, role, or active status of a Tier-2 client user

post.summary: Resend the admin invite email for an existing Tier-2 user
Resend the email-verification link for a Tier-2 client user

Phase 1 of the Unified Client Aggregate refactor — the data this endpoint writes is what Phase 5 will resolve via BillingTenantProvider in place of the soon-to-be-deleted billing.Customer row. All body fields are optional; nil leaves the existing value.

post.description: Flips Tenant.IsItalianBillable on the caller's personal tenant. Enabling requires a FatturaPA profile with CodiceDestinatario or PECDestinatario (422 otherwise); disabling is unconditional.

get.description: Lists all ingested documents with status and metadata.

post.description: Uploads a document (PDF/text), extracts text, chunks, embeds, and stores in the knowledge graph.

delete.description: Deletes a document from both MongoDB and the knowledge graph.

get.description: Returns detailed information about an ingested document.

patch.description: Updates document metadata (title, ISO standard, version) in both MongoDB and the knowledge graph.

get.description: Returns all text chunks for a document, ordered by position.
Returns cross-document SIMILAR_TO relationships for a document, showing which chunks from other documents are related.

post.description: Clears existing graph nodes for a document so it can be re-uploaded with the new pipeline.

get.description: Returns the structural section tree for a document, ordered by position.

post.description: Asks a question and returns an answer grounded in the ingested documents with source citations.

get.description: Lists all configured graph relationship types with category toggles.

delete.description: Deletes a non-system relationship type configuration.

post.description: Creates the initial developer-role user during the first-install wizard. Returns 409 Conflict once any user exists. Email verification is bypassed because this endpoint runs before SMTP can be configured.

get.description: Returns `setupCompleted` (at least one user exists) and `smtpConfigured` (notification module has real SMTP credentials). Used by the frontend SetupGate to decide whether to route to the onboarding wizard.
get.summary: Check whether the system has been initialized
List tenants the current user belongs to

post.summary: Create a new tenant (caller becomes the owner)

patch.summary: Update tenant name, slug or settings

get.summary: List role bindings in the org

post.summary: Grant a role to a user with optional expiration

get.summary: Get the current user's effective permissions in the org

patch.summary: Update a role (name/description/permissions for custom roles; isActive for any role)

get.description: Closure-table lookup of direct children (depth=1). Internal tenants never have divisions and always return an empty list.

post.description: Creates a sub-tenant (Kind=external, ParentTenantUUID=this). Refuses when the parent is internal.
post.summary: Create a division under this external tenant
Invite a user to the tenant

delete.summary: Remove a member from the tenant

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of users with optional filtering

post.description: Creates a new user with the provided information. Refuses to seed a role higher in the system tier than the caller's own (403 user.role_escalation_forbidden).

get.description: Retrieves a user by their email address
Returns the total count of users with optional filtering
Retrieves all users with a specific role

delete.description: Soft-deletes a user (alias-on-delete frees the email). Refuses self-delete (403 user.self_delete_forbidden) and last-administrator removal (403 user.last_admin_forbidden). Requires a fresh MFA proof (RequireStepUp).

get.description: Retrieves a user by their UUID

put.description: Updates a user's information. Refuses role escalation (403 user.role_escalation_forbidden) and last-administrator removal (403 user.last_admin_forbidden). Requires a fresh MFA proof (RequireStepUp).
